pain_point_label: Opaque Shared Cost Allocation & Principal Accountability

matches.match_rationale: Generating plain-language explanations of how each shared-cost line item was allocated to a site directly addresses the transparency problem.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A tool that auto-generates a budget explainer memo for each principal showing allocation details and year-over-year changes.

matches.match_rationale: Shared cost allocation data exists across multiple semi-structured sources. AI-assisted transformation can normalize these into a unified allocation schema.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An ETL layer that maps cost pools to canonical drivers and outputs a structured allocation ledger.

matches.match_rationale: Month-to-month swings in allocated amounts drive post-close disputes. Anomaly detection can flag when a site allocation deviates abnormally from trend.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A monitoring layer that flags site-cost-pool combinations where allocated amount is a statistical outlier relative to trailing 12 months.

matches.match_rationale: The choice of allocation driver is a configuration problem with multiple competing constraints. AI can enumerate valid driver combinations and score them.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An allocation design tool where finance inputs cost pools, candidate drivers, and constraints, and the system outputs a defensible driver assignment.

matches.match_rationale: Principals have no self-service way to answer why their overhead charge changed. A RAG system can let them query their cost breakdown.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A principal-facing Q&A interface grounded in allocation policy documents and historical data.
